winner.title: Complete DID method resolution algorithm per W3C DID Core §7
winner.description: spec/did-method-bolyra.md defines the DID syntax (did:bolyra:<commitment>) but lacks a conformant resolution algorithm. Implement the resolve(did, resolutionOptions) → {didDocument, didResolutionMetadata, didDocumentMetadata} interface per W3C DID Core §7.1. The resolver must query IdentityRegistry for enrollment status, populate the verificationMethod array with the BabyJubjub public key (type JsonWebKey2020 with crv=Baby-Jubjub), and set didDocumentMetadata.deactivated=true for revoked identities. Without this, did:bolyra cannot pass the W3C DID Test Suite.

outline.title: Complete DID method resolution algorithm per W3C DID Core §7

artifacts.description: Extend existing DID method spec with §7-conformant resolution algorithm: resolve(), dereference(), and error code table (invalidDid, notFound, deactivated, methodNotSupported, internalError)

TypeScript implementation of resolve(did, resolutionOptions) returning {didDocument, didResolutionMetadata, didDocumentMetadata}. Parses did:bolyra:<commitment>, queries IdentityRegistry, builds DID Document with BabyJubjub verificationMethod (JsonWebKey2020, crv=Baby-Jubjub), sets deactivated flag for revoked identities
Constructs the DID Document object: id, @context, verificationMethod array, authentication, assertionMethod. Encodes BabyJubjub public key as JWK with crv=Baby-Jubjub
Add getIdentityStatus(bytes32 commitment) view returning (bool enrolled, bool revoked, uint256 enrolledAt, bytes32 babyJubjubPubKeyX, bytes32 babyJubjubPubKeyY) to support on-chain resolution queries

Unit tests covering: valid DID resolves to correct document, revoked DID sets deactivated=true, invalid DID syntax returns invalidDid error, unknown DID returns notFound error, verificationMethod encodes correct JWK
W3C DID Test Suite conformance runner: validates resolve() return shape matches DID Core §7.1 exactly, checks all required metadata fields, runs against W3C test vectors
Developer guide: how to instantiate the resolver, configure RPC endpoint for IdentityRegistry, handle resolution errors, integrate with did-resolver universal resolver interface

steps: Audit spec/did-method-bolyra.md to inventory what DID syntax and commitment derivation are already defined; identify gaps against W3C DID Core §7.1 resolution algorithm requirements
Add getIdentityStatus() view function to IdentityRegistry.sol returning enrollment bool, revocation bool, enrolledAt timestamp, and packed BabyJubjub pubkey coordinates (X, Y as uint256)
Implement DID parser in sdk/src/resolver.ts: validate did:bolyra:<commitment> syntax, extract commitment string, return invalidDid metadata on parse failure

Implement did-document-builder.ts: construct @context array ([https://www.w3.org/ns/did/v1, crv extension context]), build verificationMethod entry with type=JsonWebKey2020 and publicKeyJwk.crv=Baby-Jubjub using X/Y coordinates from registry

Implement resolve(did, resolutionOptions) core: call IdentityRegistry.getIdentityStatus(), handle not-enrolled (notFound), build DID Document via builder, set didDocumentMetadata.deactivated=true if revoked, populate didResolutionMetadata.contentType=application/did+ld+json
Implement resolutionOptions.accept content negotiation: support application/did+ld+json and application/did+json; return representationNotSupported error for unknown types
Wire resolver to an ethers.js provider so RPC endpoint is configurable at instantiation; expose BolyraResolver class with constructor({ rpcUrl, registryAddress })
Write unit tests in sdk/test/resolver.test.ts using Hardhat in-process network: mock IdentityRegistry, test all error branches and the happy path
Write W3C conformance tests in spec/conformance/did-resolution-conformance.test.ts validating exact shape of return triple against DID Core §7.1 normative requirements
Update spec/did-method-bolyra.md with Resolution section: algorithm pseudocode, error code table, verificationMethod encoding spec for BabyJubjub JWK, and deactivation semantics
Export resolver from sdk/src/index.ts as part of public API; update sdk/QUICKSTART.md with resolver usage example
Run full test suite (npm test) and confirm all resolver tests pass; verify no regressions in circuit or contract tests
dependencies: IdentityRegistry.sol deployed with getIdentityStatus() view function
BabyJubjub public key stored in IdentityRegistry at enrollment time
W3C DID Core §7 spec (https://www.w3.org/TR/did-core/#resolution)
JsonWebKey2020 context and Baby-Jubjub JWK crv value registration
ethers.js v6 for contract interaction
did-resolver npm package interface (for universal resolver compatibility)
Hardhat local network for integration tests

winner.title: Standardize proof envelope wire format with CBOR content type negotiation
winner.description: The SDK currently passes proof objects as ad-hoc JSON with no versioning, no content type, and no canonical serialization. Define a CBOR-based proof envelope (tag 55799 self-describing CBOR) with fields: version, circuit_id, proving_system (groth16|plonk), public_signals[], proof_bytes, and an optional delegation_chain[]. Register application/bolyra-proof+cbor as the canonical media type. This is prerequisite for any HTTP-based interop — without it, every integration reinvents parsing.

outline.title: Standardize proof envelope wire format with CBOR content type negotiation

artifacts.description: Canonical specification for the application/bolyra-proof+cbor media type, CBOR tag 55799 envelope schema, field semantics, versioning policy, and content negotiation rules for HTTP transport.
CDDL (Concise Data Definition Language) schema for the proof envelope, covering version, circuit_id, proving_system enum, public_signals array, proof_bytes, and optional delegation_chain.
TypeScript implementation: encodeProofEnvelope() and decodeProofEnvelope() using cbor2 library, enforcing self-describing CBOR tag 55799, schema validation, and versioning guards.
ProofEnvelope, ProvingSystem, DelegationChainEntry TypeScript types and Zod schemas for runtime validation of decoded envelopes.
HTTP transport helpers: serializeForHttp() sets Content-Type to application/bolyra-proof+cbor, parseFromHttp() performs Accept header negotiation and falls back to JSON for backward compatibility.
Round-trip encode/decode tests for all proving systems, delegation chain presence/absence, version mismatch rejection, malformed CBOR rejection, and tag 55799 enforcement.
Canonical hex-encoded test vectors for each proving system (groth16, plonk) and delegation depth (0, 1, 2), usable by any conforming implementation.
Migration guide from ad-hoc JSON proof objects to the CBOR envelope, including backward-compat shim usage and timeline for JSON deprecation.

steps: Define the envelope field set: version (uint, starts at 1), circuit_id (tstr, e.g. 'HumanUniqueness/v1'), proving_system (tstr enum: 'groth16'|'plonk'), public_signals (array of bstr), proof_bytes (bstr, proving-system-specific encoding), delegation_chain (optional array of bstr, each a nested envelope).
Write the CDDL schema (spec/proof-envelope-schema.cddl) capturing the above, including the self-describing CBOR tag 55799 wrapper and the outer map structure.

Draft spec/proof-envelope-v1.md covering: media type registration path (application/bolyra-proof+cbor), CBOR deterministic encoding requirements (RFC 8949 §4.2 core deterministic encoding), versioning policy (major bump for breaking field changes), HTTP content negotiation (Accept/Content-Type), and interop constraints.
Add cbor2 (or cbor-x) as a dependency in sdk/package.json; audit for minimal footprint and no transitive vulnerabilities against the existing overrides policy.
Implement sdk/src/types.ts: define ProofEnvelope interface, ProvingSystem union type, DelegationChainEntry type, and Zod schemas for runtime validation.

Implement sdk/src/envelope.ts: encodeProofEnvelope() serializes to CBOR bytes with tag 55799; decodeProofEnvelope() validates tag, runs Zod schema check, version guard, and returns typed ProofEnvelope.

Implement sdk/src/http.ts: serializeForHttp() wraps encoded bytes with correct Content-Type header; parseFromHttp() inspects Content-Type and Accept, routes to CBOR or legacy JSON path, emits deprecation warning on JSON path.
Generate canonical test vectors (spec/conformance/envelope-vectors.json): one vector per proving system × delegation depth, storing hex input and expected hex output.

Write sdk/test/envelope.test.ts covering: round-trip fidelity for all proving systems, delegation chain serialization, version mismatch throws EnvelopeVersionError, missing tag 55799 throws EnvelopeFormatError, unknown proving_system string throws, oversized public_signals array handled without truncation.
Update sdk/src/index.ts public exports to expose encodeProofEnvelope, decodeProofEnvelope, ProofEnvelope type, and BOLYRA_PROOF_CONTENT_TYPE constant.
Update verifyHandshake() in sdk/src/sdk.ts to accept ProofEnvelope objects in addition to raw proof objects, delegating parsing to decodeProofEnvelope().
Write docs/proof-envelope-migration.md with before/after code snippets, the backward-compat JSON shim, and a deprecation timeline.
Run npm run typecheck and npm test in sdk/ to confirm no regressions; verify test vectors pass deterministic encoding checks.
dependencies: cbor2 or cbor-x npm package (CBOR encode/decode with tag support)
zod (already used in SDK for runtime validation)
RFC 8949 — CBOR standard (deterministic encoding §4.2)
IANA media type registration process for application/bolyra-proof+cbor (long-term; interim use of x. prefix acceptable)
Existing @bolyra/sdk v0.2.0 public API surface (no breaking changes to proveHandshake/verifyHandshake signatures)
snarkjs proof output format (groth16: {pi_a, pi_b, pi_c, protocol}; plonk: {A,B,C,...}) — must define canonical bstr encoding for each
Delegation circuit envelope nesting depth limit (recommend max depth 8 to bound CBOR size)

winner.title: Define off-chain session token format for verified handshakes
winner.description: After a successful on-chain handshake verification, there is no standardized bearer token format for the resulting session. Define a compact JWT-like session token (JWS with EdDSA over BabyJubjub) that encodes the verified nullifierHash, scopeCommitment, sessionNonce, and expiry. This enables off-chain relying parties to accept Bolyra auth results without querying the chain for every API call. Include IANA media type registration for application/bolyra-session+jwt and a COSE equivalent for constrained environments.

outline.title: Define off-chain session token format for verified handshakes

artifacts.description: Full specification: JWT header/payload/signature layout, BabyJubjub EdDSA signing algorithm identifier (crv: Baby-Jubjub), claim registry (nullifierHash, scopeCommitment, sessionNonce, exp, iss, aud, iat), and serialization rules
COSE_Sign1 equivalent for constrained environments: CBOR encoding of the same claims, algorithm mapping to ECDH-ES+Baby-Jubjub, compact binary layout
IANA media type registration template for application/bolyra-session+jwt and application/bolyra-session+cose per RFC 6838 / RFC 9290

TypeScript implementation: issueSessionToken(handshakeResult, signerKey, opts) -> compact JWS string; verifySessionToken(token, verifierKey) -> VerifiedSession; decodeSessionToken(token) -> SessionClaims (no sig check)
SessionClaims interface, VerifiedSession type, SessionTokenOptions, algorithm constants (BOLYRA_ALG = 'EdDSA-BJJ')
COSE_Sign1 variant: issueCoseSession() and verifyCoseSession() using cbor + baby-jubjub-js; mirrors JWT API surface
Round-trip tests: issue then verify, expired token rejection, tampered payload rejection, wrong key rejection, nullifierHash binding check, scopeCommitment integrity, nonce uniqueness assertion
COSE round-trip tests mirroring JWT suite; cross-format non-interoperability assertion
Normative test vectors: known nullifierHash + scopeCommitment + sessionNonce inputs -> expected compact JWS and COSE hex; used by conformance runner
Relying party integration guide: verify on-chain handshake once, cache session token, validate token on every API call without chain reads; expiry recommendations, key rotation policy

steps: Define claim set: map handshake outputs (nullifierHash, scopeCommitment, sessionNonce) to IANA-registered JWT claim names; reserve 'nul', 'sco', 'snc' as private claims under bolyra.ai namespace pending IANA registration
Specify JWT header: typ=bolyra-session+jwt, alg=EdDSA, crv=Baby-Jubjub; document that standard RS256/ES256 MUST NOT be used to prevent algorithm confusion

Define payload encoding: nullifierHash and scopeCommitment as base64url-encoded 32-byte big-endian field elements; sessionNonce as hex string; exp/iat as NumericDate; iss as DID of the Bolyra verifier contract
Specify signing procedure: serialize header.payload as ASCII bytes, sign with Baby-Jubjub EdDSA (iden3/babyjubjub), encode signature as base64url(R_x || R_y || S) — 96 bytes total
Define verification procedure: decode and validate header alg/crv, check exp > now, recompute expected nullifierHash from on-chain event log or local cache, verify EdDSA signature over header.payload
Design COSE_Sign1 mapping: CBOR label -100 for nullifierHash, -101 for scopeCommitment, -102 for sessionNonce; alg = -8 (EdDSA) with key param crv = -1 (Baby-Jubjub); full CDDL schema in spec
Implement issueSessionToken() in sdk/src/session/sessionToken.ts using noble/ed25519 shim adapted for Baby-Jubjub field; wire in existing bjj utilities from sdk/src/crypto/
Implement verifySessionToken() with constant-time signature comparison; reject tokens where nullifierHash in payload does not match the verified handshake record
Implement COSE variant in coseToken.ts using cbor2 library; ensure the two formats are never accidentally cross-validated (type discriminator check at entry point)
Write test vectors by running issueSessionToken with fixed deterministic key + known inputs; commit output to spec/test-vectors/session-token-vectors.json as normative reference
Add sessionToken exports to sdk/src/index.ts public API; bump SDK minor version to 0.3.0
Draft IANA media type registration document per RFC 6838; include contact, security considerations (replay, nullifier reuse), and interoperability notes
Update spec/draft-bolyra-mutual-zkp-auth-01.md Section 7 to reference session token format as the bearer credential produced after handshake completion
Add conformance test cases to spec/conformance/ that validate session token issuance and verification against the test vectors
dependencies: Baby-Jubjub EdDSA signing library (iden3/babyjubjub-js or noble equivalent)
cbor2 or cbor-x for COSE encoding
Existing handshake verification output types in sdk/src/types.ts
Existing BabyJubjub field utilities in sdk/src/crypto/
spec/did-method-bolyra.md (for iss DID format)
spec/draft-bolyra-mutual-zkp-auth-01.md (parent spec to update)
RFC 7519 (JWT), RFC 8037 (EdDSA in JOSE), RFC 9052 (COSE), RFC 6838 (media types)

winner.title: Publish deterministic conformance test vectors in JSON Schema
winner.description: Create a spec/test-vectors/ directory with 30+ JSON files covering: valid handshake (happy path), expired agent credential, revoked human identity, stale Merkle root, scope subset violation, cumulative bit encoding violation, delegation chain depths 1/2/3, and max-hop rejection. Each vector includes input witness, expected public signals, and expected accept/reject outcome. Validate against a JSON Schema so alternative implementations can run the suite mechanically. This is the single biggest interop enabler — no third party can implement Bolyra without reproducible test cases.

outline.title: Publish deterministic conformance test vectors in JSON Schema

artifacts.description: JSON Schema (draft-07) defining the canonical shape of every test vector: inputWitness, publicSignals, expectedOutcome, vectorId, category, circuitId
Sub-schema for HumanUniqueness+AgentPolicy combined handshake witness fields
Sub-schema for Delegation circuit witness fields including chain depth and scope bitmask
Happy-path vector: valid human+agent handshake, fresh nonce, non-expired credential, accept
Single-hop delegation with scope subset, accept
Two-hop delegation chain, each narrowing scope, accept
Three-hop delegation chain at max allowed depth, accept
Agent with READ_DATA only (bit 0), minimal permissions, accept
FINANCIAL_MEDIUM (bit 3) implying FINANCIAL_SMALL (bit 2), cumulative bits satisfied, accept
Agent credential with expiry timestamp in the past, reject
NullifierHash present in on-chain revocation set, reject
HumanMerkleRoot not present in registry's accepted roots window, reject
Delegated credential claims permissions broader than parent, reject
FINANCIAL_MEDIUM set without FINANCIAL_SMALL, encoding invariant broken, reject
Delegation chain depth 4 (exceeds max 3), reject
Reused sessionNonce from a prior accepted handshake, reject
AgentPolicy witness modelHash mismatch with on-chain registered hash, reject
Delegator attempts to sub-delegate but SUB_DELEGATE bit (6) not set, reject
ACCESS_PII (7) granted but SIGN_ON_BEHALF (5) omitted in a context requiring both, reject
One public signal field mutated post-witness-generation, proof verification fails, reject
All permission bits zero, structurally valid but semantically empty; conformance runners must accept as valid ZK proof while flagging empty scope
FINANCIAL_UNLIMITED (bit 4) set, bits 2+3 automatically implied, cumulative check passes, accept
Delegated scope equals parent scope exactly (not narrowed), boundary case, accept
Delegation at exactly depth 3 (boundary), accept; depth 4 covered by tv-015
Human-readable index: categories, how to run the suite, JSON Schema validation instructions, how to contribute new vectors
Node script: iterates all tv-*.json files, validates each against test-vector.schema.json via ajv, reports pass/fail — runnable as `node spec/test-vectors/validate.js`
Conformance runner: loads each vector, generates witness via snarkjs (fast path) or calls rapidsnark (slow path), compares actualPublicSignals and verificationResult against expectedOutcome
Normative conformance statement: which vectors are MUST/SHOULD/MAY, how third-party implementations claim conformance, versioning policy for vectors

steps: Define JSON Schema in spec/test-vectors/schema/test-vector.schema.json covering all mandatory fields: vectorId (string), category (enum: happy-path|rejection|edge), circuitId (enum: HumanUniqueness|AgentPolicy|Delegation|Handshake), inputWitness (object), expectedPublicSignals (array of stringified field elements), expectedOutcome (enum: accept|reject), rejectReason (string, required when expectedOutcome=reject)
Define sub-schemas handshake-witness.schema.json and delegation-witness.schema.json and $ref them from the top-level schema to keep witness shapes normative and independently validatable

Audit circuits/src/ and sdk/ source to extract every public signal name and ordering for HumanUniqueness, AgentPolicy, and Delegation circuits — encode signal order as a constant table in README.md and reference it in the schema description fields

Generate tv-001 through tv-006 (happy-path vectors) by running actual witness generation with snarkjs for representative inputs; capture inputWitness and publicSignals output; hand-verify accept outcome

Generate tv-010 through tv-020 (rejection vectors) by crafting inputs that trip each invariant; for ZK-layer rejections (tampered signal, encoding violation) capture the witness that would be generated and annotate why verifyProof returns false; for application-layer rejections (revocation, stale root) annotate the off-chain check that fails
Generate tv-030 through tv-033 (edge-case vectors) covering boundary conditions at scope=0, cumulative-bit implication ceiling, and max delegation depth boundary
Write validate.js using ajv@8 with draft-07 meta-schema; fail-fast on first schema violation; exit code 1 on any failure so CI can gate on it

Write runner.js that for each vector: (a) loads inputWitness, (b) calls snarkjs.wtns.calculate then snarkjs.groth16.fullProve (fast mode) or execs rapidsnark binary (slow mode via FULL_PROOF env var), (c) compares publicSignals array element-by-element using BigInt comparison, (d) calls verifyProof and compares boolean to expectedOutcome, (e) for reject vectors checks that rejectReason category matches the observed failure mode
Add `conformance:validate` and `conformance:run` npm scripts to root package.json pointing at validate.js and runner.js respectively
Add conformance jobs to CI: `conformance:validate` runs on every PR (schema check only, <5s); `conformance:run` runs on the slow CI label alongside test:circuits:slow

Write spec/CONFORMANCE.md declaring which vectors are MUST-pass for conformance claims, semantic versioning scheme for the vector suite (semver minor = new vectors, patch = fix existing, major = breaking schema change), and the process for third parties to submit new vectors via PR
Update spec/test-vectors/README.md with: directory layout, category descriptions, how to add a vector (template + checklist), and pointer to CONFORMANCE.md
Cross-reference CONFORMANCE.md from the top-level spec/did-method-bolyra.md and spec/draft-bolyra-mutual-zkp-auth-01.md so implementers discover the test suite from the normative specs
Run full suite locally, fix any signal ordering mismatches or schema gaps discovered, commit all artifacts with DCO sign-off
dependencies: circuits/src/HumanUniqueness.circom — public signal names and order
circuits/src/AgentPolicy.circom — public signal names and order
circuits/src/Delegation.circom — public signal names, chain depth signal, scope output
circuits/build/ — compiled .r1cs, .zkey, .vkey files required by runner.js witness generation
sdk/src/ — validateCumulativeBitEncoding() logic to derive reject cases for bit encoding vectors
circuits/FORMAL-PROPERTIES.md — canonical list of invariants each rejection vector must cover
spec/did-method-bolyra.md — normative anchor for revocation and Merkle root acceptance window semantics
ajv@8 (npm) — JSON Schema validation in validate.js
snarkjs ^0.7.x — witness generation and proof verification in runner.js fast path
circuits/build/rapidsnark_prover — optional binary for slow-path proving in runner.js

winner.title: Off-chain JWT session token from verified handshake
winner.description: After a successful on-chain handshake verification, emit a signed JWT containing the nullifierHash, scopeCommitment, and expiry so that LangChain/CrewAI tool chains can pass a lightweight bearer token on subsequent calls instead of re-proving. The SDK should expose `handshakeToSessionToken(proof, signingKey) → JWT` and `verifySessionToken(jwt, pubKey) → {nullifier, scope, expiry}`. This eliminates the biggest integration friction: frameworks expect sub-100ms auth checks, not 2-second proof generation on every tool invocation.

outline.title: Off-chain JWT session token from verified handshake

artifacts.description: Core session token module: handshakeToSessionToken(proof, signingKey) -> JWT and verifySessionToken(jwt, pubKey) -> {nullifier, scope, expiry}. Uses jose library for ES256 signing.
Re-export handshakeToSessionToken and verifySessionToken from public API surface.

JWT payload schema: sub=nullifierHash, scope=hex-encoded scopeCommitment, exp=expiry unix timestamp, iss='bolyra', aud=['langchain'|'crewai'|'*']. Documents signing algorithm (ES256), key format requirements, and replay-prevention via nullifier uniqueness.
Unit tests: round-trip encode/decode, expiry enforcement, tampered payload rejection, wrong key rejection, scope mismatch detection, integration with mock handshake proof output.

Integration guide for LangChain and CrewAI: one-time handshake -> token issuance -> bearer token on subsequent tool calls. Includes code snippets, token lifetime recommendations, and revocation guidance via nullifier registry.

steps: Add 'jose' as a dependency in sdk/package.json for standards-compliant JWT sign/verify (ES256, compact serialization).
Define the JWT payload TypeScript interface: { nullifier: string, scope: string, exp: number, iss: string, aud: string | string[] } in sdk/src/types.ts.

Implement handshakeToSessionToken(proof: VerifiedHandshakeProof, signingKey: CryptoKey, options?: { ttlSeconds?: number, audience?: string }) -> Promise<string> in sdk/src/session.ts. Extract nullifierHash and scopeCommitment from proof, set exp = now + ttlSeconds (default 3600), sign with ES256.

Implement verifySessionToken(jwt: string, pubKey: CryptoKey) -> Promise<{ nullifier: string, scope: string, expiry: number }> in sdk/src/session.ts. Verify signature, check exp, return structured claims. Throw typed errors on expiry or invalid signature.
Export both functions from sdk/src/index.ts alongside existing public API.

Write spec/session-token-schema.md documenting JWT structure, key requirements, recommended TTLs (3600s for agent sessions), and the security model (token is a session cache—on-chain nullifier check remains the ground truth).
Write sdk/test/session.test.ts with Mocha/Chai covering: valid round-trip, expired token rejection, tampered payload rejection, wrong public key rejection, missing claims rejection.
Write docs/session-token-integration.md with LangChain Python snippet (requests + Authorization: Bearer header) and CrewAI tool wrapper example.
Run npm run typecheck and npm run test:sdk to verify no regressions.
Update sdk/QUICKSTART.md with a 'Session Tokens' section pointing to the new integration guide.
dependencies: jose >= 5.0 (JWT sign/verify, ES256, Web Crypto API compatible)
Existing verifyHandshake() output shape (nullifierHash, scopeCommitment fields must be present)
Node 18+ Web Crypto API (SubtleCrypto) for key import — no native crypto module dependency
sdk/src/types.ts VerifiedHandshakeProof type must expose nullifierHash and scopeCommitment as top-level fields

winner.title: Standard proof envelope with MIME content type for HTTP transport
winner.description: Define an `application/bolyra-proof+json` content type and a canonical JSON envelope: `{version, circuit, publicSignals[], proof{pi_a, pi_b, pi_c}, sessionToken?, delegationChain[]}`. LangChain's `APIChain` and AutoGen's `ConversableAgent` both pass structured payloads over HTTP — without a standard envelope, every integration reinvents serialization. Add `ProofEnvelope.serialize()` / `ProofEnvelope.parse()` to both TS and Python SDKs, with round-trip test vectors in `spec/test-vectors/`.

outline.title: Standard proof envelope with MIME content type for HTTP transport

artifacts.description: RFC-style specification for application/bolyra-proof+json MIME type and canonical envelope schema
Round-trip test vectors: one HumanUniqueness proof, one AgentPolicy proof, one delegated proof with delegationChain
TypeScript ProofEnvelope class with serialize() / parse() / validate() methods and ContentType constant
Python ProofEnvelope dataclass with serialize() / parse() matching TS schema exactly
Mocha tests: serialize/parse round-trip, schema validation errors, cross-SDK vector compatibility
pytest tests: round-trip against shared spec/test-vectors/, invalid envelope rejection
Integration guide showing LangChain APIChain and AutoGen ConversableAgent usage with Content-Type header

steps: Define canonical envelope JSON schema: {version: string, circuit: 'HumanUniqueness'|'AgentPolicy'|'Delegation', publicSignals: string[], proof: {pi_a: string[], pi_b: string[][], pi_c: string[]}, sessionToken?: string, delegationChain?: DelegationLink[]}
Register MIME type constant BOLYRA_PROOF_CONTENT_TYPE = 'application/bolyra-proof+json' in both SDKs
Implement ProofEnvelope.serialize() in TS: validates required fields, JSON.stringify with stable key order, returns string
Implement ProofEnvelope.parse() in TS: JSON.parse, validates against schema, throws typed BolyraEnvelopeError on malformed input
Port ProofEnvelope to Python as a dataclass with serialize()/parse() matching TS output byte-for-byte for the shared test vectors
Write spec/test-vectors/proof-envelope-roundtrip.json with at least 3 canonical fixtures covering all circuit types
Add TS Mocha tests: round-trip fidelity against test vectors, missing-field rejection, extra-field tolerance (forward compat)
Add Python pytest tests: same round-trip fixtures, ensure json.dumps output matches TS serialize() output for each vector
Update sdk/src/index.ts and sdk-python/bolyra/__init__.py to export ProofEnvelope and BOLYRA_PROOF_CONTENT_TYPE
Add integration snippet in docs/proof-envelope-http-guide.md showing fetch() with Content-Type header (TS) and requests.post() (Python)
Verify npm run typecheck and pytest -v both pass with no new errors
dependencies: @bolyra/sdk existing proof output types (SnarkProof, PublicSignals)
snarkjs proof field naming (pi_a, pi_b, pi_c)
spec/did-method-bolyra.md for version string convention
Python bolyra package dataclass pattern from sdk-python/
Delegation circuit output schema for delegationChain link structure

winner.title: Off-chain session token with on-chain checkpoint batching
winner.description: After a successful handshake, emit a signed session token (JWT-like envelope containing humanNullifier, agentNullifier, scopeCommitment, expiry) that downstream tool calls can verify locally without hitting the chain. Batch session roots on-chain every N minutes via a Merkle accumulator. This is the unlock for agentic commerce at scale — no agent-to-agent interaction can tolerate per-call on-chain verification latency or gas. Implement in the TS SDK as `createSessionToken()` and `verifySessionToken()`, with the batching contract as a new `SessionCheckpoint.sol`.

outline.title: Off-chain session token with on-chain checkpoint batching

artifacts.description: createSessionToken() and verifySessionToken() — signs a JWT-like envelope containing humanNullifier, agentNullifier, scopeCommitment, expiry, and sessionNonce using Ed25519; returns typed SessionToken object
SessionBatcher class — accumulates session token hashes in a local Merkle tree, flushes root on-chain every N minutes via SessionCheckpoint.sol, exposes flush() and getPendingCount()
Stores batched Merkle roots of session tokens; emits CheckpointStored(root, timestamp, count); owner-controlled or permissioned submitter role
Wire format spec: header (alg, typ), payload (humanNullifier, agentNullifier, scopeCommitment, expiry, sessionNonce, issuedAt), signature; canonicalization rules for Merkle leaf hashing
Unit tests for createSessionToken / verifySessionToken: valid token, expired token, tampered payload, wrong signer, scope commitment mismatch
Tests for batching logic: accumulation, Merkle root correctness, flush trigger (time-based and manual), idempotent flush on empty batch
Hardhat tests: store checkpoint, verify root on-chain, access control on submitter, event emission
Developer guide: how to issue session tokens post-handshake, configure batch interval, verify tokens in downstream tool calls without chain access

steps: Define session token payload schema in spec/session-token-format.md: fields, canonicalization, leaf hashing (keccak256 of ABI-encoded payload)
Implement createSessionToken(handshakeResult, signingKey, expiry) in sdk/src/session.ts — produce Ed25519-signed envelope; derive leaf hash
Implement verifySessionToken(token, trustedPublicKey) in sdk/src/session.ts — check signature, expiry, and scopeCommitment consistency
Build incremental Merkle accumulator in sdk/src/sessionBatcher.ts — insert leaf hashes, compute root via keccak256 binary tree
Add flush() method that submits Merkle root to SessionCheckpoint.sol via ethers.js; support configurable batch interval (default 5 min) and max batch size (default 100)
Write SessionCheckpoint.sol — mapping(uint256 => bytes32) checkpointRoots, storeCheckpoint(bytes32 root, uint256 count) with submitter role, CheckpointStored event
Compile and deploy SessionCheckpoint.sol to local Hardhat node; update deploy scripts for Base Sepolia
Wire SessionBatcher into the SDK's public API — export from sdk/src/index.ts alongside existing createHumanIdentity, createAgentCredential
Write unit tests for session.ts (happy path + all failure modes)
Write unit tests for sessionBatcher.ts (accumulation, flush, empty-batch guard)
Write Hardhat integration tests for SessionCheckpoint.sol
Add end-to-end test: full handshake → createSessionToken → verifySessionToken → batch flush → on-chain root verification
Write docs/session-tokens.md developer guide with code examples
Run tsc --noEmit and npm test; fix any type errors or test failures
DCO sign-off on all commits
dependencies: Existing proveHandshake / verifyHandshake API in sdk/src/index.ts
sessionNonce binding already enforced in handshake — reuse as session token nonce
ethers.js (already in sdk/package.json) for on-chain flush
@noble/ed25519 or @noble/curves for session token signing (lightweight, audited)
Hardhat + contracts/hardhat.config.ts for SessionCheckpoint deployment
pot16.ptau and existing circuit build artifacts (no new circuits required)
Base Sepolia RPC + deployer key in contracts/.env for testnet deploy

winner.title: Prove cumulative bit encoding constraints are necessary and sufficient
winner.description: The Delegation circuit enforces cumulative bit invariants (bit 4 implies bits 2+3, bit 3 implies bit 2) on the delegatee scope. Formally verify two properties: (1) SOUNDNESS — no witness satisfying the circuit constraints can violate the implication rules, and (2) COMPLETENESS — every valid cumulative encoding is accepted. Write a standalone test harness that exhaustively checks all 2^8 permission byte values against the circuit constraints and confirms exact agreement with the SDK's validateCumulativeBitEncoding(). This also catches any off-by-one in bit indexing between Circom (LSB-first Num2Bits) and the Solidity/TS layer.

outline.title: Prove cumulative bit encoding constraints are necessary and sufficient

artifacts.description: Standalone sub-circuit that encodes exactly the cumulative-bit implication rules (bit4→bits2+3, bit3→bit2) as Circom constraints, importable by Delegation.circom and independently testable.

Exhaustive harness: iterates all 256 permission byte values, computes expected validity via SDK validateCumulativeBitEncoding(), generates a witness for each value against CumulativeBitCheck, and asserts exact agreement. Failures surface bit-indexing mismatches between Circom LSB-first Num2Bits and the TS/Solidity layer.
Reference to (or minor audit of) the existing validateCumulativeBitEncoding() function — the ground truth for the completeness check. No changes expected; function is treated as oracle.

Append two formally stated properties: SOUNDNESS (no satisfying witness violates implication rules) and COMPLETENESS (every valid encoding is accepted). Include table mapping Circom bit indices to permission names.
Document how to run the completeness harness, what a failure means, and how to extend when new permission bits are added.

steps: Audit Delegation.circom: extract the exact Circom constraints that enforce cumulative-bit implications; note the Num2Bits signal ordering (LSB = bit index 0 = READ_DATA).
Audit sdk/src/permissions.ts validateCumulativeBitEncoding(): document which bit indices it checks and the exact implication rules it applies; confirm LSB vs MSB convention matches Circom.
Create circuits/src/CumulativeBitCheck.circom as a standalone template(1 input: permByte[8] bits) that encodes only the implication constraints; add assert-style IsZero/IsEqual gates for each rule.
Wire CumulativeBitCheck into Delegation.circom via component instantiation so the Delegation circuit delegates (pun intended) its scope-validation to the sub-circuit — single source of truth.

Write circuits/test/cumulative_bit_completeness.test.js: for each v in 0..255, (a) call validateCumulativeBitEncoding(v) to get expected boolean, (b) attempt witness generation on CumulativeBitCheck with input bits derived from v, (c) assert witness success iff SDK returns valid.
Run the harness in fast mode (witness-only, no proof) to get the exhaustive 256-case result; log any disagreement with full bit breakdown.

Fix any discovered mismatches: if a Circom constraint is too tight (rejects valid encoding) or too loose (accepts invalid), correct CumulativeBitCheck.circom; if SDK is wrong, fix validateCumulativeBitEncoding().
Re-run full 256-case sweep to confirm zero disagreements.
Append SOUNDNESS and COMPLETENESS properties to circuits/FORMAL-PROPERTIES.md with the bit-index table and reference to the exhaustive test as the proof artifact.
Update circuits/test/README.md with run instructions and a note that any new permission bit requires extending both CumulativeBitCheck.circom and the SDK validator in lockstep.
dependencies: circom_tester (npm, dev)
snarkjs ^0.7.x
@bolyra/sdk (validateCumulativeBitEncoding as oracle)
circuits/src/Delegation.circom (existing cumulative-bit constraints to audit)
circuits/build/pot16.ptau (only needed for slow/proof mode, not for witness-only sweep)
